name: Stubb's Waller Creek Amphitheater
parking: No dedicated lot. Use street meters (free after 6 PM/12 AM) or Hotel Indigo/Holiday Inn Express Garage at 9th & Red River. Rideshare recommended.
age_limit: Generally All Ages (under 17 needs guardian). Some shows 18+ or 21+.
vibe: Quintessential Austin. Dusty, sloped outdoor dirt floor (wear boots!). Sloped ground means great sightlines even from the back. Famous BBQ attached.
tips: Cashless venue. Outdoor show, check weather.

name: Mohawk
parking: Very limited street parking on Red River. Use St. David’s Church Garage or paid lots south of 6th St.
age_limit: Varies. Many All Ages, but late-night events often 21+. Check ticket link.
vibe: Indie heaven. Multi-level with indoor and outdoor stages. Balconies offer the best views in the city. Raw, authentic, and loud.
tips: Cashless venue. Check both inside and outside stage schedules.

name: Emo's Austin
parking: Do NOT park in adjacent shopping center lots (instant tow). Use street parking on Royal Crest Dr or Burton Dr.
age_limit: Usually All Ages. Strict ID checks for 21+ wristbands.
vibe: Professional black-box warehouse. Standing room only, big-tour feel, top-tier sound system.
tips: Cashless venue. High energy, can get very crowded.

name: Moody Center
parking: Expensive ($40-$60). Pre-book Manor or Trinity Garage. Bullock Museum lot is a cheaper 15-min walk away.
vibe: Modern, massive (15k capacity), air-conditioned. Incredible acoustics. Local food vendors like H-E-B and Stubb's inside.
tips: Cashless venue. Bag policy is strict (clear bags only).

name: Empire Control Room & Garage
parking: Street parking on E. 7th or nearby paid lots. Rideshare easier due to one-way streets.
vibe: Industrial/Experimental. Intimate 'Control Room' with immersive visuals + open-air 'Garage'. Go-to for electronic, hip-hop, and psych-rock.

name: Scoot Inn
parking: Limited street parking. Official garage at 501 Navasota St (one block north).
vibe: Established 1871. Cozy, string-lit backyard party vibe. Great for Americana, indie, and folk.
tips: Cashless venue. Outdoor venue, very relaxed atmosphere. FOOD: Usually has a resident food truck (like Arlo's or similar) on the patio, and is walking distance to many East 6th eateries.

name: The Ballroom at Spider House
parking: Aggressive towing area! Small lot behind venue fills fast. Street parking on W 29th/30th is resident-only/permit heavy. Rideshare highly recommended.
age_limit: Usually 18+ or 21+. Strictly enforced ID policy.
vibe: Eclectic 'Old Austin'. 1920s bungalow meets punk dive bar. Intimate indoor stage with retro decor and a large breezy patio shared with Tweedy's Bar.
tips: Check out the food trucks on the patio. Extremely casual dress code. Arrive early if you want a table inside.
